phrasal_verbs.meaning: visit quickly or for a short time
take sb on a tour of a place
put on fancy or unusual clothes
repeat or think about again in order to understand completely
develop from; become too big for
put on (a piece of clothing) to see how it looks and whether it fits
give something valuable to your children or grandchildren, usually when you die

A.title: Topic vocabulary in contrast
A.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

questions.sentence: Architects often argue that a building's ...... is just as important as its practical function.
That dark wooden floor doesn't really ...... the pale walls in this room.
The interior designer prefers a ...... aesthetic, favouring clean lines and minimal decoration.
The museum has a fascinating section dedicated to historical ...... from different cultures.
As the curator walked past, we caught a ...... of the new painting before it was hung.
The ...... trend in interior design is to blend vintage pieces with contemporary furniture.
We need to find a completely ...... approach to marketing this collection.
The bespoke frame didn't ...... the canvas exactly, so the artist had to adjust it slightly.
The sculptor used a special ...... to polish the bronze surface of the statue.
Bold geometric patterns might not ...... everyone's taste in home decoration.
The gallery director ...... at the portfolio and said it showed real creative promise.
The minimalist ...... is particularly popular in Scandinavian design circles.
Do you think the **average / everyday** person truly appreciates abstract art?
I think a lighter, simpler frame would **costume / suit** that painting far better.
Many traditional dancers prefer to put **on / wear** their costumes on stage rather than backstage.
The photographer wore a plain white **blouse / top** to avoid distracting attention from the subject.
This studio **supplies / produces** art materials to schools and colleges across the region.
The stage backdrop was **dyed / painted** in deep blues and greens to create an underwater atmosphere.
The **design / manufacture** of handmade ceramics demands extraordinary skill and patience.
The theatre company is looking for an experienced **costume / suit** designer for their new production.

C.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
C.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: If you ask me, it's high time they (1) ...... away with all these rigid rules about what counts as real art. I've had enough of it!
Every time I pop (2) ...... a gallery, I feel like an outsider for not having studied art formally.
One minute you're expected to take all the complexity (3) ...... and strip everything right back, and the next you have to have your techniques handed (4) ...... from the traditional masters.
You can't win! Well, I've torn (5) ...... all my old art theory textbooks and adopted what I call instinctive creativity.
It's a philosophy that has grown (6) ...... of years of frustration and, honestly, a lack of confidence to just express myself freely.
I started by drawing (7) ...... a personal creative manifesto.
I decided to line all my canvases (8) ...... against the wall, try a completely new style (9) ...... for each one, and donate to local schools anything that felt complete.
Then I promised myself I would not start anything new until my current sketchbook had worn (10) ....
I went (11) ...... my manifesto one final time and felt completely ready to begin.
At last, I had the courage to start. It felt a little like dressing (12) ...... for the theatre - stepping into an entirely new version of yourself.
And if you ever come to my studio and I (13) ...... you around the space, you might see unconventional methods, but you'll also see an artist who feels truly free.
And that's instinctive creativity. Do you think it'll catch (14) ......?
I always enjoy ...... on different artistic styles before committing to one for a major project.
A bold colour scheme ...... combination with natural textures can make any living space feel warm and welcoming.
Everyone complimented the architect ...... her innovative use of natural light in the new building.
The auction house described the sculpture as one of the most impressive ...... of art sold this decade.
I think some people ...... a tendency to dismiss street art without ever really looking at it carefully.
Classic architectural forms will never go ...... of style, no matter how design trends evolve.
When restoring an old painting, you should always ...... the protective varnish in thin, even coats.
The upholstery has a bold ...... pattern that clashes completely with the striped wallpaper.
You work far too rigidly. Why don't you just ...... your hair down and paint whatever you feel?
The current trend ...... architecture is to use sustainable and locally sourced materials wherever possible.
My colleague and I share exactly the same taste ...... design, which is why we collaborate so well.
Why not ...... your mentor's example and submit your work to the national design competition?
My tutor asked me whether my design approach was ...... fashion, and I told him it was ahead of its time!

E.title: Word patterns (gap fill)
E.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: It was the young artist's first major solo show and she was quite anxious ...... how the critics would react.
The installation seems ...... have been inspired entirely by the artist's early childhood memories.
I couldn't help staring ...... the enormous mural that covered the whole side of the building.
Before finalising the logo design, the team referred ...... several examples of classic typography for inspiration.
Plenty ...... self-taught artists have achieved remarkable success without any formal training.
I asked the gallery curator to advise me ...... which pieces would best suit my private collection.
Several critics have praised the photographer ...... her groundbreaking and distinctive approach to portraiture.
The lead designer insisted ...... using only locally sourced and sustainable materials throughout the project.
Many studios now use specialised three-dimensional software ...... visualise their designs before anything is built.
Attending open studio events can be very useful ...... meeting other creative professionals in the industry.

F.title: Verb patterns
F.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verb in brackets.

questions.sentence: She was very proud ...... (see) her photographs displayed in such a prestigious international gallery.
Her mentor advised her ...... (study) graphic design at the institute, despite her parents' reservations.
To succeed as a professional illustrator, you have to be prepared ...... (work) to very tight deadlines.
The artist knew there was still plenty ...... (do) before the opening night of the exhibition.
After a frank discussion with the gallery owner, Sofia's manager advised her against ...... (become) a full-time commercial artist.
The director's latest documentary doesn't seem ...... (be) as inventive as the remarkable films he produced earlier.
When Elena first started working as a designer, she was always anxious ...... (please) every single client.
The tutor advised the students ...... (forget) about copying established styles and to develop their own creative voice instead.

G.title: Word formation (text)
G.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

questions.sentence: I was really (1) (ENTHUSE) ...... about my first ever visit to a major international art gallery.
I had always loved art but had only ever seen famous works in (2) (ADVERTISE) ...... for museums and auction houses.
Finally, I was going to see real masterpieces in person! I put on what I hoped were (3) (FASHION) ...... clothes for the occasion.
I wanted to look as (4) (STYLE) ...... as the gallery assistants who work there.
I'm not entirely sure I was completely (5) (SUCCESS) ...... in that aim, but I felt (6) (ATTRACT) ...... enough, which is what matters.
When we arrived, the gallery itself was extraordinary - filled with (7) (BEAUTY) ...... works from every period of art history.
The guided tour began, and imagine my astonishment when, completely (8) (EXPECT) ......, I found myself face to face with a painting that looked remarkably like one of my own sketches!
Several visitors noticed the (9) (SIMILAR) ...... immediately, and two of them jokingly asked me who my personal (10) (STYLE) ...... was!

H.title: Word formation (sentences)
H.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

questions.sentence: This sculptor's latest series is really quite ...... (LIKE) anything she has ever created before.
Handcrafted furniture is extremely ...... (DESIRE) these days and can sell for very high prices.
I couldn't choose between the two designs - but then again, I have always been quite ...... (DECIDE).
We visited the ceramics studio and observed part of the ...... (PRODUCE) process from raw clay to finished piece.
I'm afraid I'm completely ...... (USE) when it comes to reading technical architectural blueprints.
I don't ...... (LIKE) abstract art - I simply find it harder to interpret than figurative painting, that's all.
These watercolours are ...... (BEAUTY) executed - just look at how delicately the light is captured in each one!
I'm still ...... (DECIDE) about whether to apply for the graphic design course or the fine arts degree.
